mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates broad economic strength and robust corporate earnings potential. This positive fundamental signal drives equity appreciation over a medium-term horizon as earnings reports confirm the trend.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS value of 150263.0 reflects continued job additions.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient of 3-month changes in PAYEMS with 60-day forward returns is consistently below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month rate of change in CPI accelerates past a critical threshold, it signals that inflation is becoming entrenched, forcing the central bank into a more aggressive tightening path than the market anticipates, causing a negative repricing of equities.
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following a trigger event, the S&P 500 posts a positive return in more than 50% of instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous downturn in retail sales (consumer spending) and housing starts (investment spending) provides a powerful, joint signal of a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, leading to lower expected corporate profits and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: A model using the interaction of RSAFS and HOUST fails to show a statistically significant improvement in predictive power (via likelihood ratio test) over single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of low unemployment, any significant uptick in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a strong leading indicator of a cyclical peak and impending recession (the 'Sahm Rule' principle), causing a rapid de-risking and negative market returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.3pp from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 3.9% is historically low, making any increase more salient.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (correctly predicting negative returns) is not statistically higher inside the specified low unemployment regime compared to outside of it.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises or accelerating trends in industrial production reflect a robust supply side of the economy, translating quickly into higher revenue expectations and positive short-term momentum for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is actively raising the FEDFUNDS rate while inflation (CPIAUCSL) continues to accelerate, it signals a 'behind the curve' policy error. This combination implies future policy will be even more restrictive, severely compressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly lower than the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP is the textbook definition of an economic slowdown. This backward-looking but definitive data point confirms weakening fundamentals, causing investors to revise future earnings down and sell equities.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive returns in the 60 trading days following the release of a negative GDP report in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, a weakening labor market (rising UNRATE) is particularly damaging for equities because it signals an impending recession at a time when the central bank's ability to provide monetary stimulus is constrained.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.27% represents a restrictive policy stance.
failure_condition: The negative performance of the S&P 500 following a rise in unemployment is not statistically more severe when FEDFUNDS is above 2.0% compared to when it is below 2.0%.
